title: waltheria longiramea
intro: Waltheria longiramea is a plant species commonly known as the West African walnut, belonging to the family Malvaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits associated with Waltheria longiramea include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
